title: 8 Pieces Rubber Jar Gripper Pads Round Kitchen Coasters Multi-Purpose Bottle Lid Openers, Multicoloured (Bright Color)
description:
Rubber bottle lid openers: round jar gripper pads are home and kitchen tool for opening bottles, tight containers, jars much easier
Multicolor: the color of round jar pads is for you to choose as your preferences, enough colors for your various selection
Non Slip Material: rubber material, 5 inches/ 12.3 cm in diameter, the size is suitable for most sizes of people hands
Multi-purpose: they are not only applied for opening jars and bottles easily, but also can work as round kitchen coasters, anti scald pads; Note: when opening the bottle with a bottle opener, please ensure that the opener is dry to make it easier to open the jar and bottle
Package: comes with 8 pieces non slip jar opener pads, sufficient quantity and colors for you to choose or replace in daily life; on receipt, the product can be washed with water and then left to dry in a ventilated area the opener will separate and become soft
reviews_5s:
These gripper pads are very nice! They are as pictured, a very nice size, pretty variety of colors, and they work amazingly well. I have ordered multiple sets and will order more when needed. I do recommend these for opening jars, and slippery objects...they do work! Side note some may have been damaged but nothing Amazon was not willing to rectify. Overall they are very very nice and I would definitely recommend this set!I was skeptical at first because the texture is very smooth versus the ones I have used in the past that had a rough texture. The smoothness has a sticky-like coating that sticks to the lids to give you an even better grip than before. They also feel very durable and imagine they would last a long time. Great product and Great price!These grippers really do their job of helping you open stuck jar lids because the plastic tends to adhere well so you can use a lot of torque without slippage. They have an odd sticky texture, which I wouldn’t like to hold except when I need a non-slip surface.

I use fountain pens, and I fill them on the counter in my bathroom. The problem is that glass ink bottles are slippery and so is the counter top. Enter the jar gripper. I put it down on the counter, put the ink bottle on top, and nothing slips. I can fill my pens with out worrying about a disaster if I push the bottle too hard and it slides off the edge.

Good product for my real use, and also for opening jars.This has helped me open jars that even my incredibly strong and muscular dad couldn't open, and i'd say that's a MAJOR WIN! I also just use them if I need a good grip on something. Great product!!!These are very thin and I don't know how long they will last, but they grip like crazy. On a container I was ready to cut open because I couldn't get the contents any other way, I put one of the grips on the cap and one grip on the container, and I was able to twist it open. I couldn't believe it. Didn't take all my strength either. Perhaps being thin is part of how they work, allowing the force from your hands to be applied very directly. When you get the package, the grips are all stuck together in what seems almost like a solid mass. You have to carefully peal them apart to use them.I love these, nothing negative to say about them. They work perfectly, sometimes I use two (one to hold the bottle or whatever it is I'm using it to unscrew a top off of and one on the lid/top) because I need a better hold on it if it's slippery, really stuck on there, or the bottle is large. If I use one to open something that was oily or greasy on the outside, a quick wash with a little blue Dawn dish soap to get the residue off and it's back to new. They're handy because they stick on the fridge, cabinet doors, and mirrors (it's kinda fun to just chuck them on haha). I just cleaned those surfaces before putting them there and they don't fall off so they're always right there and handy.

I use one in the tray of my car because it's shallow and things like to slide around and out of it. It's kept that from being an irritating little thing and I can use it now.

I think remember seeing questions asking if these have latex or not. I can say that no, they do not for a few reasons. If they did, I'd know. I have a severe allergy to latex and have an anaphylactic reaction at even slight contact with it. They don't smell like latex, and they would have broken or become weak using them on oily things (oil breaks latex down). I think they're made out of silicone or they feel like that.I purchased these to use as a cushion between dishes in our boat cupboard. When I first opened the package, I was afraid that the discs had been fused together somehow (heat/sun on the package?), but they were perfectly fine! Just know that they are a bit difficult to pull apart the first time. My second concern was that they might stick the plates together, but that was not the case either. They did exactly as I hoped - they keep they plates from sliding around in the cabinet when we are underway, but allow me to easily separate the plates when I need them. They are perfectly grippy and just the right thickness. Bonus is that I can also use them as a hotplate and to protect my hands from hot pot handles. I even ordered a second set!Strong durable and all colorsThese dots are great. I purchased them to use for opening bottles and ended up using them on my vehicle. There were a bunch of us traveling to our camp by vehicle and we where following each other. So we used some of the dots on our vehicles so we could easily identify each other on the high way. They worked really good. We just now need to find some of these that are more florescent to be seen easily at night and during the day. Something the sun won't wash out but can bee also seen in the dark.These work well, and it's nice to have such a wide assortment of colors. I put one in each of my suitcases and one in a box of grill tools that I carry on trips to AirBNB cabins.

The only quibble I have with these is that they were stuck together really hard, and it was initially difficult to separate them from each other in the stack. (Would have been nice if the packaging included a slip of waxed paper between each of them. ) The first time I needed to use one, I actually used THREE stuck together. Worked just as effectively that way, though, so no problem!
reviews_1s:
These don’t grip. There’s no texture at all. They are completely smooth rubber pieces. They stick onto each other very well, but won’t stick or grip to much else.

I tried to open a screw top wine bottle: won’t grip the top.

Tried to keep a glass from sliding on table with condensation: nope.

Tried to just stick one on a piece of wood so a few items on that shelf wouldn’t scratch the back board: nope.

These were a waste of time and cash.

These also attract the most minimum of dust…& I’m in a place with over 80% average humidity…so why? Static isn’t a thing here! Shake anything immediately out of the dryer and the static is gone. So what gives with these things? I’m always cleaning the dust off both sides of the stuck discs.No gripper ridges. But this also means the vinyl (or whatever it is) discs fuse TIGHTLY together! I suspect, out of laziness, they assemle all eight sheets at once, and then stamp them out as a stack. However they do it, they manage to deliver a single chunk of 8 discs fused tightly together. I spent the first 10 minutes just trying to pull all eight apart! How dumb! A cheap sheet of tissue or wax paper between each disc would make this far easier to use. But that could add a few pennies to this already overpriced product. So I proudly give this product one star across the board.Really odd material and they stick together, hard to separate. No wonder they were shipped with paper between each one. Too thick and no gripping ability. Threw them away. Waste of money.These jar grippers are a joke. They are smooth and slippery and literally have to be peeled off each other in order to use them. There is no texture to do the gripping. They tightly stuck together and when stored next to one another stick tightly together again, making them hard to separate when you want to use them.Complete waste of money. There's no grip to the rubber material so it really does NOT help you open a jar or bottle -- incredibly frustrating, leaves your hand aching, and still item not open! I basically have tossed all of them and will get something with texture and grip. :(These are made from a very smooth rubber that creates a vacuum between them. I couldn't separate them by hard, and was unable to slip a knife between them without cutting them. After trying for too long. I threw them away. A waste of money."8 rubber jar grippers" arrived completely melted together--in winter. Now it's a hard thick rubber pancake. The price isn't worth my time to return, yet the purchase is time and money I won't see again.I can’t pull them apartI’m sorry I bought these. They came stuck together and I couldn’t get them unstuck. I was also disappointed that they weren’t like the other one I had.Sorry, this one was a miss for me. Very cheaply made. They are very “sticky” as opposed to others that known which have an actual “gripper” texture. I’m worried they will stick to everything in the drawer. Disappointed.
